DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
token
style
pattern

DSDS Guideline Definitions

A single, unified guideline definition used across all DSDS artifact types. Every guideline is a self-contained, actionable statement paired with a rationale. Optional properties allow guidelines to express external criteria, content targets, and categorization without requiring separate types.

Source: common/guideline.schema.json

guideline

A single, self-contained piece of usage guidance. Guidelines appear in flat arrays on artifact documents. The optional `category` property groups by discipline (e.g., visual-design, accessibility, content). The optional `target` property identifies which anatomy part or content area the guideline applies to. The optional `criteria` property links the guideline to external functional requirements via URLs. The optional `type` property classifies the guideline's enforcement level.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
guidancerichTextrequired
rationalerichTextrequired
type"required" | "encouraged" | "informational" | "discouraged" | "prohibited"optionalThe enforcement level of the guideline, classifying how strictly it should be followed. This property answers the question: 'What kind of guideline is this?' Values align with RFC 2119 requirement levels: 'required' (MUST — non-compliance is a defect; use for accessibility mandates, platform constraints, or system invariants), 'encouraged' (SHOULD — follow in most cases; exceptions require justification; use for well-established best practices), 'informational' (MAY — advisory context with no enforcement expectation; use for tips, background, or emerging patterns), 'discouraged' (SHOULD NOT — avoid unless justified; use for practices with known drawbacks), 'prohibited' (MUST NOT — violations are defects; use for patterns that break accessibility, semantics, or system integrity). When omitted, consumers MAY treat the guideline as 'encouraged'.
categorystringoptionalThe discipline or domain this guideline belongs to. Used to group guidelines by professional concern. Standard values: 'visual-design' (color, typography, spacing, layout), 'interaction' (behavior, animation, transitions, gestures), 'accessibility' (inclusive design, assistive technology, WCAG compliance), 'content' (text, imagery, tone, voice, labeling), 'motion' (animation timing, easing, reduced-motion), 'development' (implementation patterns, performance, code conventions). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case. Category identifies the discipline; use `tags` for cross-cutting topics that span multiple disciplines.
targetstringoptionalThe anatomy part or content area this guideline applies to (e.g., 'label', 'icon', 'helper-text'). Typically used when category is 'content'. When omitted, the guideline applies to the artifact as a whole.
criteriastring (uri)[]optionalURLs to external functional requirements or standards that this guideline addresses. Generalizes beyond WCAG to any published criteria — accessibility standards, platform guidelines, regulatory requirements, or internal compliance documents. For WCAG criteria, use the direct URL to the success criterion (e.g., 'https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum'). For platform guidelines, link to the specific section (e.g., 'https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/accessibility').
Format: uri
examplesexample[]optionalExamples illustrating the guideline.
tagsstring[]optionalFreeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation').

References: richText, example
